body:
![high](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/high-priority.svg)

Setting `--note-cell-size` to `24px` here will cause a visual misalignment with the instrument info panel in `InstrumentRow.tsx`, which is hardcoded to `h-10` (40px). To maintain a cohesive layout, the instrument info panel height should also be made dynamic or updated to match this new cell size.

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

The specification in `docs/PRINT_PDF_LIVE_SPEC.md` (Line 26) requires all print output to use black borders for high contrast. Using `#ddd` for note cell borders violates this requirement and may reduce clarity on physical paper.

```suggestion
      border-color: black !important;
```

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

Using `Date.now()` for generating unique IDs can lead to collisions if multiple instruments are added in very rapid succession. Using `crypto.randomUUID()` is a more robust approach for generating unique identifiers in modern web applications.

```suggestion
                      id: `inst-${crypto.randomUUID()}`,
```

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

The default value for `--note-cell-size` here (40px) differs from the fallback used in `GridColumnLabels` (24px). These should be synchronized to ensure the grid remains aligned even if the CSS variable is missing. Given the project's existing `h-10` (40px) standard, 40px is a safer default for consistency across components.
Addressed. Updated `InstrumentRow.tsx` to use the `--note-cell-size` CSS variable for visual alignment.
Addressed. Updated `app/print.css` to use black borders for all note cells in print mode.
Addressed. Replaced `Date.now()` with `crypto.randomUUID()` for instrument ID generation.
Addressed. Synchronized `GridColumnLabels` to use the CSS variable for both width and height with a 40px fallback.
Addressed. Synchronized all component fallbacks for `--note-cell-size` to 40px.

`GrooveGridEditor` now hard-codes `--note-cell-size: 24px` for *all* usages (including the interactive editor), while other parts of the grid still assume ~40px row height (e.g., the instrument panel uses `h-10`). This can cause visible vertical misalignment and an unintended global UI shrink outside of print/readOnly views.

**AI-Optimized Suggestion:** Update `components/groove/GrooveGridEditor.tsx` to set `--note-cell-size` conditionally (e.g., `readOnly ? '24px' : '40px'` or via an explicit prop), and align row/header heights to the same CSS variable so the instrument panel and note cells stay the same height.

Grid wrapping is newly introduced via `MEASURES_PER_ROW`/`rowCount` and the `startNoteIdx`/`endNoteIdx` slicing, but the unit tests don’t currently assert the new multi-row behavior (e.g., measures=3 should render 2 grid blocks and render only 2 measures worth of cells per block).

**AI-Optimized Suggestion:** Add/extend a test in `components/groove/__tests__/GrooveGridEditor.test.tsx` that renders a grid with `measures: 3` (and notes/velocities sized accordingly) and asserts: (1) two grid blocks are rendered, and (2) each block contains the expected number of `note-cell` elements (2 measures per block, then 1 measure in the last block).

diff_hunk:
@@ -8,64 +8,75 @@
     .no-print,
     [role="button"],
     input[type="checkbox"],
-    select {
+    select,
+    [data-testid="editor-toolbar"] {
body:
`[data-testid="editor-toolbar"]` is referenced in the print-hide selector list, but there is no `data-testid="editor-toolbar"` in the codebase (search shows it only exists here). This makes the intent brittle and potentially ineffective.

**AI-Optimized Suggestion:** In `app/print.css`, remove the unused `[data-testid="editor-toolbar"]` selector and replace it with a stable hook (e.g., add a `.no-print`/`data-print-hide` attribute on the toolbar component and target that instead).
```suggestion
    select {
```

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

The `--note-cell-size` is hardcoded to `24px`, which overrides the default `40px` defined in `NoteCell`. This change affects all instances of the `GrooveGridEditor` globally, including the main editing interface where larger touch targets are typically preferred. Consider making this a prop to allow different sizes for the chart view versus the editor view.

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

The `MEASURES_PER_ROW` constant is hardcoded to `2`. While this matches the current print specification, making this a configurable prop would improve the component's reusability for different screen sizes or layout requirements (e.g., a 'compact' vs 'wide' view).

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

`crypto.randomUUID()` is only available in secure contexts (HTTPS). If the application is accessed over HTTP or in certain legacy environments, this will throw an error. Providing a fallback ensures the 'Add Instrument' functionality remains reliable.

```suggestion
                      id: "inst-" + (typeof crypto !== 'undefined' && crypto.randomUUID ? crypto.randomUUID() : Date.now()),
```
/gemini are you able to mark comments as Resolved once they've been fixed?

As an AI assistant, I do not have the capability to modify the status of comments or mark them as resolved in the GitHub UI. That action must be performed by you or another collaborator with the appropriate repository permissions.

`GridBody` now renders one `overflow-x-auto` scroll container per wrapped row. On narrow viewports this will create multiple independent horizontal scrollbars and let rows get out of horizontal alignment (labels/notes no longer line up across rows), which is especially problematic when editing or comparing measures.

AI-Optimized Suggestion:
In `components/groove/GrooveGridEditor.tsx`, make horizontal scrolling a single shared container (e.g., wrap the whole set of row blocks in one `overflow-x-auto` element and remove `overflow-x-auto` from each block), or explicitly sync `scrollLeft` across row containers so all rows stay aligned.

`handleDragEnd` is guarded by `isDraggingRef.current`, but this ref is never set to `true` anywhere in the file. As written, the global `mouseup` listener and selection-apply logic are effectively dead code (and the listener is still installed on every mount), which is confusing and makes drag-to-apply behavior impossible if it’s intended to work.

AI-Optimized Suggestion:
In `components/groove/GrooveGridEditor.tsx`, either (a) remove `isDraggingRef` + the `mouseup` listener + `handleDragEnd` selection-apply logic if drag-to-apply is no longer supported, or (b) set `isDraggingRef.current = true` when a drag selection starts (e.g., from `NoteCell`/`InstrumentRow` onMouseDown via context or a callback) so `handleDragEnd` can run.

`orderList` uses `manualOrder || ...`, which treats an intentionally provided empty string as “no override” and falls back to the auto-generated order. If `manualOrder` is meant to be an override value, this should use nullish coalescing so `''` is respected.

AI-Optimized Suggestion:
In `components/chart/SongChartHeader.tsx`, compute `orderList` with `manualOrder ?? sections.map(...).join(', ')` (and optionally `.trim()` the final string) so an explicit empty string doesn’t get overwritten by the auto-generated order.
```suggestion
  const orderList = manualOrder ?? sections.map((s) => s.name).join(', ');
```

Print styles target `.grid-editor-container`, but there’s no element in the repo using this class. As a result, the intended print-specific grid border/break styling won’t apply.

AI-Optimized Suggestion:
In `app/print.css`, replace `.grid-editor-container` with a selector that matches the actual grid root (e.g., `[data-testid="groove-grid"]`), or add `className="grid-editor-container"` to the GrooveGridEditor root in `components/groove/GrooveGridEditor.tsx` so the existing print rules apply.

The print CSS neutralizes `.bg-surface-container-low` / `.bg-surface-container-high`, but the grid mostly uses opacity variants like `bg-surface-container-low/20`, `bg-surface-container-high/10`, and `bg-surface-container-low/50`. Those classnames won’t match the current selectors, so dark-theme surface backgrounds can still bleed into print output (reducing the intended black-on-white contrast).

AI-Optimized Suggestion:
In `app/print.css`, add explicit overrides for the opacity-variant classes used by the grid (e.g., `.bg-surface-container-low\/20`, `.bg-surface-container-high\/10`, `.bg-surface-container-low\/50`) to set `background: transparent !important`, while keeping the special-case beat highlight override for `.bg-surface-container-high\/40`.
```suggestion
    .bg-surface-container-high,
    .bg-surface-container-low\/20,
    .bg-surface-container-low\/50,
    .bg-surface-container-high\/10 {
```

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

The visual regression test for the Groove Grid is being skipped. While the PR mentions manual auditing, automated visual regression is critical for catching layout shifts in the grid wrapping logic across different resolutions and browsers. Please provide a reason for skipping or update the baseline screenshots to reflect the new wrapped layout.

`NoteCell`’s interactive `<button>` appends `bg-transparent` after `containerClasses`, which overrides the conditional beat/selection background classes in `containerClasses` (Tailwind conflict resolution uses the last `bg-*`). This makes cells lose beat shading and selected highlighting.

**AI-Optimized Suggestion:** In `components/groove/NoteCell.tsx`, remove `bg-transparent` from the button `className` (or move it before `containerClasses`) so the `bg-surface-container-*` and `bg-primary/20` classes can apply as intended.
```suggestion
        className={`${containerClasses} appearance-none p-0 focus:outline-none focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-primary focus-visible:ring-inset rounded-sm w-full h-full`}
```

`rowCount` is computed as `Math.ceil(measures / measuresPerRow)`. If `measuresPerRow` is `0` or negative (prop misuse), this becomes `Infinity`/`-Infinity` and `Array.from({ length: rowCount })` will throw.

**AI-Optimized Suggestion:** In `components/groove/GrooveGridEditor.tsx`, clamp/validate `measuresPerRow` (e.g., `const safe = Math.max(1, measuresPerRow | 0)`) before using it in `rowCount`, `startMeasure`, and `endMeasure`.

With grid wrapping, multiple rows now render with the same `data-testid` (e.g., multiple `instrument-row-kick`), which forces tests to use regex + `.first()` and makes selectors ambiguous.

**AI-Optimized Suggestion:** In `components/groove/InstrumentRow.tsx`, incorporate `rowIndex` into `instrumentRowTestId` when provided (e.g., `instrument-row-kick--row-0`) or add a second deterministic test id, so each rendered row is uniquely addressable without relying on `.first()`.

In the paste test, the selection is ended with `fireEvent.mouseUp(window)`, but `GridBody` now listens for `mouseup` on `document`. This can leave selection/drag state inconsistent and make the test pass/fail for the wrong reason.

**AI-Optimized Suggestion:** In `components/groove/__tests__/GrooveGridEditor.test.tsx`, change `fireEvent.mouseUp(window)` to `fireEvent.mouseUp(document)` anywhere the code under test uses a `document` mouseup listener.
```suggestion
        fireEvent.mouseUp(document);
```

`pasteData` uses `notes: ['accented']`, but the grid’s `DrumSymbol` values use `accent` / `accent_opt` (see `NoteCell`’s `symbolToIcon`). Using a non-existent symbol in this test can mask real paste behavior and would break icon rendering if it ever reached the UI.

**AI-Optimized Suggestion:** In `components/groove/__tests__/GrooveGridEditor.test.tsx`, change the pasted symbol from `'accented'` to the valid `'accent'` (or another valid `DrumSymbol`), so the test reflects real clipboard data.
